zh.lifeSummary: 當Herman Magnusson Fleming till Villnäs於1440年出生在芬蘭瓦爾希乃-素門 馬阿昆塔馬斯庫阿斯凱時，他的父親Magnus Klasson Fleming40歲，母親Elin Nilsdotter Kurck40歲。他於1470年與Elin Filipsdotter在芬蘭結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1495年在芬蘭瓦爾希乃-素門 馬阿昆塔阿斯凱享年55歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Herman Magnusson Fleming till Villnäs于1440年出生在芬兰瓦爾希乃-素門 馬阿昆塔馬斯庫阿斯凱时，他的父亲Magnus Klasson Fleming40岁，母亲Elin Nilsdotter Kurck40岁。他于1470年与Elin Filipsdotter在芬兰结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1495年在芬兰瓦爾希乃-素門 馬阿昆塔阿斯凱享年55岁。

nameMeanings.name: Fleming
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: ethnic name for someone from Flanders, from Middle English fleming. The word reflects a Norman French form of Old French flamanc ‘Fleming’, from the stem flam- + the ancient Germanic suffix -ing. In the Middle Ages there was considerable commercial intercourse between England and the Netherlands, particularly in the wool trade, and many Flemish weavers and dyers settled in England. This surname is also common in south and east Scotland and in Ireland, where it is sometimes found in the Gaelicized form Pléamonn.
German: variant of   Flemming  , cognate with 1 above.

variants.name: Flemings
meanings: English: plural form of   Fleming  .
Americanized form of Flemish Vleminckx: patronymic from Vleminck, an ethnic name for someone from Flanders, Middle Dutch vleminc.

variants.name: Flanders
meanings: English: ethnic name from Middle English Fla(u)ndres ‘Flanders’, denoting someone from Flanders, a historical region covering parts of modern-day Belgium, France, and the Netherlands. Compare Fleming .

variants.name: Fleeman
meanings: English: variant of   Fleming  .

variants.name: Flemming
meanings: German: ethnic name for someone from Flanders, Middle High German vlaeminc. People from Flanders spread throughout Germany, as well as England, in the Middle Ages as clothmakers and dyers.
English: variant of   Fleming  .

variants.name: Flamenco
meanings: Spanish: ethnic name from flamenco ‘Fleming, Flemish person’ (see Fleming ).

nameMeanings.name: till
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English female personal name Till, a pet form of Old French Matild(e) (in its Latin form Matilda), which was an alternative to Maud; see Maude 2. A diminutive form occurs in Tillotson .
North German: variant of   Thiel  .

variants.name: Tilly
meanings: German: from a shortened form of Tilling, which itself is a patronymic from Till .
English and Irish: variant of   Tilley  .
Irish: variant of   Tully  .

variants.name: Tyl
meanings: Polish and Czech: from the personal name Tyl, a Slavicized form of German Till and Thiel 1. The same Sorbian surname, which may also be from a short form of the personal name Bartyl ‘Bartholomew’ (see Barthel ), is found only in a Germanized form, Thiel 2.

variants.name: Maude
meanings: English (of Norman origin):

habitational name from Mold in Flintshire, Wales, which name derives from Anglo-Norman French mont haut ‘high hill’ (Latinised as mons altus), the Norman name for Bailey Hill on which the castle stood. The name de Montalt, de Mohaut was borne by an Anglo-Norman aristocratic family who acted as castle wardens there but who had their chief seat in Riddlesden in Yorkshire, where the surname proliferated as Mawhood and Maude. In the 17th century one branch of the family acquired lands in Ireland. The Maude baronetcy of Dundrum, Tipperary, was created in 1705 for Robert Maude. His son Cornwallis Maude, Baron de Montalt, was created first Viscount Hawarden in 1793. Mowatt is the Scottish form of the name, from a different branch of the same family.

occasionally from Middle English and Norman French female personal name Mahald or Maud, which are Old French forms of ancient Germanic Mahthild (Latinised as Matilda), composed of the Germanic elements maht ‘might, power’ + hild ‘battle’. This personal name partly owed its great popularity among Normans in England to the fact that it was the name of the wife (c. 1031–83) of William the Conqueror. Surnames derived from this personal name are found in many forms and spellings, for example, Mould and Moll .

variants.name: Tillmann
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Fritz, Ruedi, Franz, Georg, Helmut, Joerg.  Scandinavian Holger, Johan.
North German: elaborated form of Till , with the addition of -mann ‘man’.

variants.name: Tillett
meanings: English (East Anglia): from the Middle English female personal name Tillot, a diminutive of Till , itself a pet form of Matill (Matilda). Compare Tillotson .

variants.name: Tillis
meanings: English (Norfolk): variant of Tills, itself a variant of Till , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.
Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): variant of   Tilles  .

nameMeanings.name: Villnäs
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Nestor, Alejandro, Carmelita, Jorge, Julio, Luz, Manuel, Orlando, Rafaela, Roque.

Spanish: habitational name from a place called Villas in Cáceres (Extremadura) or Las Villas in northern Spain, named with the plural of villa ‘(outlying) farmstead, (dependent) settlement’ (see Villa ).

variants.name: Villa
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Manuel, Francisco, Jesus, Carlos, Luis, Mario, Miguel, Raul, Pedro, Ramon.  Italian Antonio, Lorenzo, Angelo, Dario, Federico, Luciano, Dante, Leonardo, Marco, Mauro, Salvatore, Vito.

Spanish: topographic name from villa ‘(outlying) farmstead, (dependent) settlement’ (see 2 below), or a habitational name from any of the places (mainly in Asturias) called Villa, or from any of numerous places called with this as the first element. Compare Billa and Lavilla .

Italian: topographic name for someone who lived in a village as opposed to an isolated farmhouse, or in a town as opposed to the countryside, from Latin villa ‘country house, estate’, a term later used to denote a group of houses forming a settlement and in some dialects to denote the most important area or center of a settlement. This may also be a habitational name from any of various places called with villa. Compare Davilla .

nameMeanings.name: Herman
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of Hermann , from a Germanic personal name derived from heri, hari ‘army’ + man ‘man’. The name was in use among the Normans and was borne by many immigrants from the Low Countries in the 15th century. Perhaps because of that it continued in occasional use well into the 1700s. It was revived more generally in Britain in the 19th century, when it also became common in America, most probably as a result of the influence of German immigrants.

nameMeanings.name: Magnusson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Niels, Anders, Erland, Gisli, Kerstin, Lars, Nils, Ottar, Thor.  German Hans, Kurt, Fritz.
Swedish: patronymic from the personal name   Magnus  .
Americanized form of Danish, Norwegian, or North German   Magnussen  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Magnus
meanings: Originally a Latin byname meaning ‘great’, this was first extracted from the name of Charlemagne (recorded in Latin chronicles as Carolus Magnus ‘Charles the Great’) and used as a given name by the Scandinavians. It was borne by seven medieval kings of Norway, including Magnus I ( 1024–47 ), known as Magnus the Good, and Magnus VI ( 1238–80 ), known as Magnus the Law Mender. There are several early Scandinavian saints called Magnus, including an earl of Orkney ( d. 1116 ), to whom Kirkwall cathedral is dedicated. The name was imported to Scotland and Ireland during the Middle Ages.

variants.name: Magnuson
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Nels, Helmer, Sven, Kerstin, Alf, Anders, Hilma, Knut, Monrad, Thor.
Americanized form (and a rare Swedish variant) of Swedish   Magnusson  .
Americanized form of Danish, Norwegian, and North German   Magnussen  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Manson
meanings: pet form of   Marie  , common in the 18th and 19th centuries, and now also used in the English-speaking world. The name is familiar in the English-speaking world through the  Abbé Prévost's  story Manon Lescaut (  1731 ), which was given operatic treatment by both Puccini and Massenet. In it the young Chevalier des Grieux elopes with the heroine, who supports them by becoming a courtesan and is eventually deported to Louisiana, where she dies.

en.lifeSummary: When Herman Magnusson Fleming till Villnäs was born in 1440, in Askainen, Masku, Varsinais-Suomen maakunta, Finland, his father, Magnus Klasson Fleming, was 40 and his mother, Elin Nilsdotter Kurck, was 40. He married Elin Filipsdotter in 1470, in Finland. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. He died in 1495, in Askainen, Varsinais-Suomen maakunta, Finland, at the age of 55.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Herman Magnusson Fleming till Villnäs est né en 1440, à Askainen, Masku, Varsinais-Suomen maakunta, Finlande, son père, Magnus Klasson Fleming, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Elin Nilsdotter Kurck, avait 40 ans. Il a épousé Elin Filipsdotter en 1470, à Finlande. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé en 1495, à Askainen, Varsinais-Suomen maakunta, Finlande, à l’âge de 55 ans.

Er hat 1470 in Finnland Elin Filipsdotter geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb 1495 im Alter von 55 Jahren in Askainen, Varsinais-Suomen maakunta, Finnland.

Lui sposato Elin Filipsdotter nel 1470, nel Finlandia. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1495, nel Askainen, Varsinais-Suomen maakunta, Finlandia, all'età di 55 anni.

ko.Willnäs, Moisio, Turku Pori, Finland: 핀란드 터르쿠 자 포리 터르쿠 모이시오
ko.lifeSummary: Herman Magnusson Fleming till Villnäs(이)가 1440년 핀란드 바르시나이스-수멘 마악운타 마스쿠 아스카인에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Magnus Klasson Fleming(은)는 40세였고, 그의 어머니 Elin Nilsdotter Kurck(은)는 40세였다. 그는 1470년 핀란드에서 Elin Filipsdotter(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1495년 핀란드 바르시나이스-수멘 마악운타 아스카인에서 55세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Herman Magnusson Fleming till Villnäs nasceu em 1440, em Askainen, Masku, Varsinais-Suomen maakunta, Finlândia, seu pai, Magnus Klasson Fleming, tinha 40 anos e sua mãe, Elin Nilsdotter Kurck, tinha 40 anos. Ele casou-se com Elin Filipsdotter em 1470, em Finlândia. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1495, em Askainen, Varsinais-Suomen maakunta, Finlândia, com 55 anos.

ru.Willnäs, Moisio, Turku Pori, Finland: моисио, турку, турку джа пори, Финляндия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Herman Magnusson Fleming till Villnäs родился в 1440 г. в местности аскеинен, маску, варсинеис-суомен маакунта, Финляндия, его отцу по имени Magnus Klasson Fleming было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Elin Nilsdotter Kurck, 40. Он женился на женщине по имени Elin Filipsdotter в 1470 г. в местности Финляндия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер в 1495 г. в местности аскеинен, варсинеис-суомен маакунта, Финляндия в возрасте 55 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Herman Magnusson Fleming till Villnäs nació en 1440, en Askainen, Masku, Varsinais-Suomen maakunta, Finlandia, su papá, Magnus Klasson Fleming, tenía 40 años y su mamá, Elin Nilsdotter Kurck, tenía 40 años. Se casó con Elin Filipsdotter en 1470, en Finlandia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1495, en Askainen, Varsinais-Suomen maakunta, Finlandia, a la edad de 55 años.
